reasoning: Measured _regeneratePatchedJWKs() cost on main@a623eab by invoking setPatches([]) after populating the JWKManager's _observedIssuers to N in {10, 20, 40, 80} via onOracleEvent (pranked as NATIVE_ORACLE). Observed gas is 4.86M / 10.60M / 24.86M / 64.46M respectively — a 13.27x growth for 8x N-increase, which is clearly super-linear (linear would give 8.0x exactly) with an accelerating per-doubling ratio of 2.18 → 2.34 → 2.59 (linear=2.0, quadratic=4.0) that confirms the quadratic term gradually dominating. At N=80 the cost already exceeds the 30M block gas limit by >2x, so in production every onOracleEvent would OOG inside NativeOracle's try/catch (NativeOracle.sol:314) while the nonce has already been consumed (NativeOracle.sol:85) and JWKManager state changes rolled back — permanent DoS. Governance's escape hatch setPatches also routes through the quadratic body. The literal '30x by N=80' threshold suggested in the task is not achievable until N ≈ 200 because a sizeable linear term (~430k gas per issuer for the sorted-insert search and JWK-copy) dominates at small N; however, the vulnerability manifests long before that ratio appears. Test PASSES three assertions: super-linear (ratio_80_10 >= 12x), accelerating doubling ratio (quadratic signature), and absolute gas > 30M (DoS on real block). Dynamic verdict agrees with static EXISTS.
